rationale: Obligation requires proving post1_self_ == post2_self_ (StrictlyOrderedMap<K>) from view-level postconditions of an external_body `set` fn. StrictlyOrderedMap contains keys: StrictlyOrderedVec<K> (which wraps Vec<K>) and vals: Vec<ID>. Vec<T> SMT-equality in Verus is opaque: two Vecs with identical views (Seq) are NOT necessarily ==. The ensures only constrain self@ (=m@), gap, and valid(); they leave the actual Vec internals unconstrained. Best-effort proof block (revealed closed specs, used =~= / =~~= on Map/Set, derived view-level equalities) hits two unprovable gaps: (1) sorted+no_duplicates+same to_set ==> keys@ == keys'@ needs an inductive helper lemma — but inserting new fn/proof fn items inside the proof body is forbidden; (2) even with keys@ == keys'@, deriving keys.v == keys'.v (and vals == vals') requires a Vec view-eq axiom that does not exist in vstd (group_vec_axioms / lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq concern eq_spec, not SMT ==). Companion case `delegation_map_v__impl4__new__new`
